note: Light-doc submodule of sitewide_alert. Recipe-only cases probe the one thing this submodule adds: a placeable 'Sitewide Alert' block (plugin id sitewide_alert_block) so alerts render in a region/Layout Builder instead of the default top-of-page injection. No site mutation.

evals.prompt: In the Sitewide Alert project, which submodule lets me render alerts inside a chosen theme region or Layout Builder section instead of forcibly at the top of every page, and how does it do it?
evals.expected_output: The sitewide_alert_block submodule: it provides a 'Sitewide Alert' block plugin you place in a region via Block layout or Layout Builder, instead of the parent module's default top-of-page injection.

evals.prompt: After enabling the Sitewide Alert Block submodule, what is the machine name / plugin id of the block I place, and where do I place it?
evals.expected_output: The block plugin id is sitewide_alert_block (admin label 'Sitewide Alert'); place it in a theme region at /admin/structure/block or in a Layout Builder layout.

evals.prompt: Does Sitewide Alert Block replace the alert entities and settings, or reuse them? What does it depend on?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the parent sitewide_alert module and reuses the same alert entities, styles, scheduling and dismissal — only the placement (a block) changes.
